title: OutpostDef
description: Static definition for a barbarian outpost camp archetype — a standalone, world-gen-only structure guarded by a fixed-strength garrison, owned by BaseSiteSelector.NEUTRAL_OWNER_ID. Destroyed outright (no capture, no ruin) once both its tower and its garrison are dead, at which point whoever dealt the tower's killing blow is granted `loot`. See sim/outposts/. Live per-match state (BarbarianOutpostInstance) is NOT part of this schema.

id.description: Unique identifier for this camp archetype, e.g. "barbarian_camp_wood".

buildingType.description: A data/buildings/*.json id with isStandalone:true — V1 always reuses "tower" rather than authoring a barbarian-specific building.

material.description: Must be a key in buildingType's materialStats. No fallback: an empty or non-matching material silently resolves to a zero-HP, free, harmless building (see sim/bases/building_stats.gd _hp_model), so this is required rather than optional.

garrison.description: Standing troops guarding the tower, same shape/semantics as BaseDef.initialGarrison (data/bases/schema.json) — seeded directly onto the map at world-gen via GarrisonFactory, independently owned so they never change hands (there is no capture for outposts, only destruction).

loot.description: One-time resource grant to whoever destroys the tower, paid out once the garrison is also cleared (in either order) — see BarbarianOutpostLootSystem.
